title: sideritis glacialis subsp. virens
intro: Sideritis glacialis subsp. virens, also known as the Greek mountain tea or Alpine sideritis, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Sideritis. It is an herb that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been used to promote respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and cold. Sideritis glacialis subsp. virens can be consumed as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with the tea being the most popular herbal preparation.
